behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behavior for scroll items: U8P_BASESCRL::look displays a short descriptive text/bark when the player examines the scroll, and U8P_BASESCRL::use activates the scroll by spawning the runtime process that implements the scroll's effect.

Both U8P_BASESCRL::look and U8P_BASESCRL::use determine the scroll type by calling Item::getQuality(this) and U8P_BASESCRL::use dispatches to specific SCROLL1::* or SCROLL2::* handler processes based on that numeric quality/type ID.

The U8P_BASESCRL::look handler maps several concrete quality IDs to human-readable short texts (examples: 0x01 -> 'scroll of dispelling magic portals', 0x32–0x37 -> trap/healing/invisibility related strings) and falls back to a generic 'scroll ' string when no specific mapping is found.

The U8P_BASESCRL::use handler maps many IDs to specific SCROLL1::funcXXXX handlers and a small group of IDs to SCROLL2::* handlers; when no match is found it spawns a default handler (SCROLL1::func0080). Both handlers U8P_BASESCRL::use suspend/pauses around messages to allow on-screen display or effect timing.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BASESCRL::look: read Item::getQuality(this) -> select string via switch-like mapping -> display bark and suspend to allow message viewing.

U8P_BASESCRL::use: store implicit this -> call Item::getQuality(this) -> switch-like dispatch on numeric ID -> spawn target handler process (SCROLL1::* or SCROLL2::*) with addressof(item) as target -> suspend.
Both functions U8P_BASESCRL::use a default/fallback path when the quality/type ID is not recognized (U8P_BASESCRL::look shows generic 'scroll ', U8P_BASESCRL::use spawns SCROLL1::func0080).
open_questions: Complete numeric-to-handler mapping: the provided excerpt includes many mappings but not necessarily the full set of quality IDs and their SCROLL1/SCROLL2 handler targets — what are the full ID→effect mappings?
What gameplay semantics exactly correspond to each quality ID (e.g., are quality IDs reused to encode rarity vs effect) beyond the example mappings in the decompiled labels?

Are there any side-effects (inventory changes, consumption) performed by the spawned SCROLL1/SCROLL2 handlers that callers of U8P_BASESCRL::use should be aware of (the current script only spawns the handler and suspends)?

main_function.name: U8P_BASESCRL::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short on-screen description/bark for a scroll item when examined by the player by reading the item's quality/type ID and selecting an appropriate description string.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game engine when the player uses the 'U8P_BASESCRL::look' or 'examine' action on the scroll item.
Triggered by the item's U8P_BASESCRL::look/examine event (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_BASESCRL::look
function_map.role: Display short description/bark for scroll items based on Item::getQuality(this).

function_map.name: U8P_BASESCRL::use
function_map.role: Activate/Use the scroll: read quality ID and spawn the corresponding SCROLL1/SCROLL2 handler to perform the scroll's effect.

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: Query the item's numeric quality/type ID that discriminates scroll kind and behavior.

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's numeric quality/type ID used to select the descriptive bark string.
Read the item's numeric quality/type ID used to choose which scroll-effect process to spawn.
Start the runtime process that implements the scroll's effect; the selected SCROLL1 or SCROLL2 handler is chosen by the quality/type ID and receives the item address as its target.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behavior for scroll items: U8P_BASESCRL::look displays a short descriptive text/bark when the player examines the scroll, and U8P_BASESCRL::use activates the scroll by spawning the runtime process that implements the scroll's effect.
